claim: Computer-use agents require proper initialization of action spaces and tool definitions; incomplete setup leads to failures even with capable underlying models.
actionable: Ensure all tool actions are properly registered and validated before deploying computer-use agents; missing initialization is a common failure point.
source_title: OpenClaw Doesn't Work Until You Do This

claim: Computer-use agent success depends on providing clear action feedback loops so the agent understands the result of each action.
actionable: Implement feedback mechanisms (success/failure signals, visual confirmation) for each action the computer-use agent takes.
source_title: OpenClaw Doesn't Work Until You Do This
